title: craspedia richea var. macrocephala
intro: Craspedia richea var. macrocephala, also known as Billy Buttons, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its bright yellow globular clusters of tiny flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Australia and New Zealand, for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The health benefits of Billy Buttons are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, Billy Buttons can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices, and is often combined with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
